publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 17, 2025 at 23:19

did.unittitle: Hohenzollern-Schlaberg-Hughes collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of artwork, photographs, correspondence, government documents, and printed material relating to the House of Hohenzollern and its descendants.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Artwork, 1519-1929, undated. II. Correspondence and Photographs, circa 1860s-2007. III. Printed Material, 1735–1993. IV. Other Papers, 1986, undated.

bioghist.head: House of Hohenzollern
bioghist.p: The House of Hohenzollern was a German royal family whose members were princes, electors, kings and emperors of Hohenzollern, Brandenburg, Prussia, the German Empire, and Romania. The Hohenzollerns were overthrown when the Weimar Republic was established in 1918. Family members include Frederick William I, King of Prussia; Frederick II, King of Prussia; Kaiser Wilhelm II; and Charles Frederick Schlaberg II. Several family members, such as Prince August Wilhelm and Prince Alexander Ferdinand of Prussia, were supporters of the Third Reich. American descendants include Dr. Thomas Hughes and Thomas Lowe Hughes, a Yale Law School graduate and Director of the Bureau of Intelligence and Research during the Kennedy and Johnson administrations.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hohenzollern-Schlaberg-Hughes Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of artwork, photographs, correspondence, government documents, and printed material relating to the House of Hohenzollern and its descendants. Artwork contains portraits of Frederick II, King of Prussia, and German Emperor William II, maps, and genealogical charts by artists including Dominicus Custos, Albrecht Dürer, and Anton Schutz., as well as correspondence, such as letters from Frederick II during the Seven Years' War to military commanders such as General Hans Karl von Winterfeldt. The collection also contains letters to Thomas Hughes and Thomas Lowe Hughes from various family members, including Prince August Wilhelm and Prince Alexander Ferdinand, both Nazi Party supporters, who wrote during World War II. Among the other correspondents are Kaiser Wilhelm II and Armgard Weygand, Prince Alexander Ferdinand's wife.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Hohenzollern-Schlaberg-Hughes Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains lithographs, etchings, engravings, and photographs of Hohenzollern family members including William II and Frederick II, as well as scenes of German palaces and cities, correspondence and military documents, maps, and family trees.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

did.unittitle: Friedrich Wilhelm, Elector of Brandenburg. Document, signed, with a 19th century steel-engraved portrait and a facsimile page
Frederick William I, King of Prussia. Military commission in the Königlichen Regiment zu Fuss, signed with an engraved portrait
Frederick II, King of Prussia. Letter, signed, to General Hans Karl von Winterfeldt, with colored lithograph portrait
Frederick II, King of Prussia. Autograph letter, signed, to Major von Bruening, with a 19th century colored, engraved portrait
Artist unknown. View of Berlin Castle, engraving
Prince August Ferdinand of Prussia. One letter in French and one letter in German signed "your affectionate friend," with an engraved portrait
Prince August Wilhelm of Prussia. View from the Prince's window in Schloss Langenburg, watercolor, signed with monogram and inscribed Langenburg Sept. '48
Photographer unknown. Knochenhaueramtshaus on the Markplatz
Photographer unknown. Altdeutschenhaus, the Schlaberg-Behrens house
View of Jagdschloss Grunewald, Berlin, copperplate engraving signed with initials E.H.X.A.
Levering, Albert. Satiric view of the Kaiser from Life magazine
Photographer unknown. William II, German Emperor, sitting on a park bench at Doorn (silver gelatin print)
Photographer unknown. C, photographic postcard
Atelier Sandau. William II, German Emperor, autographed portrait, inscribed and dated Doorn. 11.II.39 (silver gelatin print)
Schutz, Anton Joseph Friedrich. Schloss Sans Souci , etching, signed, titled, and inscribed in pencil
Dürer, Albrecht. Cardinal Albrecht of Brandenburg; or, The Small Cardinal , engraving
Dürer, Albrecht. Cardinal Albrecht of Brandenburg; or, The Great Cardinal , engraving
Von dem Teutschen Landt . Page from book with an account of the Brandenburg electors, pedigree of Joachim II, and a list of his children including Sigismund
Plate XV (engraving) from "Art Heraldique" showing the Royal arms of twelve European sovereigns
Winckler, Jean Benoît. Hand-colored engraving of Rheinsberg
Artist unknown. View of Sans Souci from the rear entrance and yard, hand-colored engraving
Artist unknown. Prospect of the formal gardens of Sans Souci, hand-colored engravings
Thornton [first name unknown]. A View of the City of Berlin , copperplate engraving for Millar's New Complete & Universal System of Geography
Carmine, Joseph. Frederick the Great, en route to the Battle of Torgau, pair of hand-colored engraved vues d'optiques
Benning, R. The Front of the Palace of the King of Prussia , hand-colored engraving
Benning, R. The Palace of the King of Prussia , hand-colored engraving
Artist unknown. Five color lithographs of Hohenzollern-related buildings and settings from Berlin and Potsdam
William I, German Emperor. Military commission signed by William as king and countersigned by Otto von Bismarck, with royal seal, accompanied by colored steel engraved portrait
Seitz, I. Engraving with portraits of the first nine Hohenzollern archbishops and administrators of Magdeburg
Artist unknown. Black and white print of 16th century royal scene depicting the Hohenzollern household, possibly 19th century facsimile
Artist unknown. Black and white print of 16th century royal scene, possibly 19th century facsimile
Regnier, C. Jos. Nikolaus Graf zu Hohenzollern, color lithograph
Artist signature illegible. View of Burgrave's Castle (Hohenzollern Castle), Nüremburg, etching
Steiger, E. Black-and-white photograph of William II, German Emperor, "signed and colored by His Majesty," with a letter from the Kaiser's Court Marshal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter thanks Dr. Thomas Lowe for the two volumes on Abraham Lincoln by Albert Beveridge which Dr. Lowe sent to the ex-Kaiser for his 70th birthday.

did.unittitle: Artist signature illegible. Hand-colored etching depicting the Berlin Schloss viewed from the Spree
Schutz, Anton Joseph Friedrich. Sans Souci, Park, Orig. ***, Signed A. Schutz and titled in pencil, lower margin, etching
Chereau, Jacques. View of Koenigstsein, hand-colored engraving
Boettger [first name unknown] Depicting palaces of Frederick William III, King of Prussia
Fournier [?] "Entreé triomphante des Francais dans la ville de Berlin," colored engraving
Frederick III, German Emperor, autograph letter, signed, with a 19th century steel engraved portrait
Hildebrandt, Carl [?] Brandenburg coat of arms (large and small), with titles and lists of Hohenzollern territories, color lithograph
Frederick William II, King of Prussia, letter, signed, with an oval engraved portrait by J. Poss
Poppel, Johann. Hand-colored engraving depicting the Charlottenburg Schloss
[Poppel, Johann?] Hand-colored engraving depicting the Berlin Schloss
Wolf, Theodore. Hand-colored engraving for Le Monde depicting King William I and his family visiting the Hohenzollern castle in Hechingen
William II, German Emperor. Military document, signed, with a portrait photograph
Custos, Dominicus. Brandenburg-Hohenzollern genealogical chart, showing descent from the 12th century counts of Zollern, engraving
Attributed to Dominicus Custos. Genealogical Hohenzollern family tree up to the time of Joachim Frederick, Magdeburg, in 1566, hand-colored engraving
M. Rösler sculpsit. Hand-colored engraving depicting the procession of the French ambassador reaching Frankfurt-am-Main, from the Holy Roman Emperor's coronation book
Rittner, Heinrich. Vue occidentale de la forteresse de Koenigstein en Saxe , after C. G. Hamer, colored engraving
William I, German Emperor. Military commission, signed, to Pehlemann, with an offset reproduction portrait
William II, German Emperor. Patent document, signed, with a colored portrait
Hohenzollern family tree
Rosenberg, Jean. Vue de la Place d'Armes
Rosenberg, Jean. Passage de Chateau a la Ville Neuve
Correspondence and Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence to Thomas Lowe Hughes and Dr. Thomas Lowe from other members of the Hohenzollern family. Included are letters from Prince August Wilhelm, a supporter of the Nazi party, and envelopes marked "checked" in German with the seal of Oberkommando der Wehrmacht. The series also contains several photograph albums with images dating from the 1860s to 1880s.

did.unittitle: Letters, photos, and cards to Dr. Thomas Lowe and Thomas Lowe Hughes
Album I. Kaiser Wilhelm II and Kaiserin Hermine
Album II. Crown Prince Wilhelm, Crown Princess Cecilie, Prince Frederick, Prince Eitel Frederick, Prince Adalbert, Princess Adelheid, Princess Victoria Louise
Letters, photos, and cards to Thomas Lowe Hughes
Album III. Description and photocopies of selected letters
Album III. Louis Ferdinand, Prince of Prussia, and Kira Kirillovna, Grand Duchess of Russia
Album IV. Description and photocopies of selected letters
Album IV. Prince August Wilhelm
Album V. Prince Alexander Ferdinand of Prussia and Princess Armgard Weygand
Album VI. Description and photocopies of selected letters
Album VI. Prince Alexander Ferdinand of Prussia and Princess Armgard Weygand
Album VII. Prince Oskar, Princess Ina Marie, Princess Burchard, Princess Eleonore, Princess Herzeleide, Prince Biron of Curland
Album VIII. Description and photocopies of selected letters
Album VIII. Prince Wilhem-Karl and Princess Armgard Weygand
Photograph Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cartes de visite studio portraits of the Prussian royal family. Primarily Kaiser Wilhelm I, Friedrich III, and Wilhelm II.

did.unittitle: Photograph Album
Printed Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains postcards, clippings, and other published ephemera, as well as several books.

did.unittitle: Unidentified book, printed in German
Postcard albums

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also included are a small amount of original photographs.

did.unittitle: Volume I. Early Hohenzollerns and Kaiser Wilhelm II and family
Volume II. Crown Prince Wilhelm & family; Prince Eitel Frederick; Prince Adalbert
Volume III. Prince August Wilhem & family; Prince Oscar & family; Prince Joachim & family; Princess Victoria Louise
Published photographs
Volume I: Emperor and Empress Frederick III; William II - Youth
Volume II: Emperor William II
Volume III: Emperor William II
Volume IV: William II - World War I
Volume V: William II - Doorn
Volume VI: Crown Prince William and his family
Volume VII: The younger children of William II; Prince Henry of Prussia and his family

title.part: Bernardo Bellotto : The Fortress of Königstein

did.unittitle: Other Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains one color slide, a newspaper clipping, and assorted labels for artwork and books in the Hohenzollern collection.

did.unittitle: Other papers
